message:
🐛 Fix ksc_error auth spike from 7 new marketplace card hooks (#9933) (#9953)

The 7 marketplace card PRs (#9921–#9928) introduced hooks whose fetchers
only treated HTTP 404 as 'not installed'. On Netlify the MSW catch-all
returns 503 for unknown /api/ routes, and the JWT middleware returns 401
for unauthenticated visitors — both caused unhandled errors that surfaced
as ksc_error events with error_code: auth.

Apply the same defensive pattern used in the TiKV fix (#9923):

1. NOT_INSTALLED_STATUSES — treat 401, 403, 404, 501, 503 as 'endpoint
   unavailable' and return empty/not-installed data instead of throwing.
2. Defensive JSON parse — wrap resp.json() in try/catch so Netlify's SPA
   fallback (text/html 200) doesn't produce 'Unexpected token <' errors.
3. Optional-chaining on response body fields.

Affected hooks:
- useCachedDapr   (/api/dapr/status — no backend handler)
- useCachedSpiffe (/api/spiffe/status — no backend handler)
- useCachedTuf    (/api/tuf/status — no backend handler)
- useCachedOtel   (/api/mcp/custom-resources)
- useCachedRook   (/api/mcp/custom-resources)
- useCachedDragonfly (/api/mcp/custom-resources)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
